# Reconciliation Report: planning suite vs current PM scripts

## Summary
- Checks: 13/13 pass in `planning.als`; 5/5 pass in `planning_lease.als`; 1/1 pass in `planning_plan_race.als`. Total **19/19 pass**.
- Scenarios: 11 SAT + 2 expected-UNSAT in `planning.als`; 3 SAT + 1 expected-UNSAT in `planning_lease.als`; 1 expected-UNSAT in `planning_plan_race.als`. All match expectations.
- Source artifacts compared: code (`skills/pm/scripts/*.py`, `schema_fragment.json`), spec (the model itself), skill prose (`skills/pm/*/SKILL.md`), README, prior enforcement report.
- Discrepancies found: 4 (1 Aligned post-migration with mechanism shift, 2 FixSource on stale prose, 1 Exclusion already documented).
- Enforcement (Aligned items): all gates remain Enforced; the load-bearing gate just relocated from PM-script-level (deterministic-text collision) to storage-layer (`chain_predecessor` head-move check).

## Drift trigger

The just-landed migration on `main` swaps the claim-race mechanism:

| | Before | After |
|--|--|--|
| `prevStatus` schema role | plain `single` link | `chain_predecessor: true` |
| Race resolution | `append_claim` writes deterministic text `claim:<task>/<prev>`; second writer collides on `text_sha256` | hashharness compares-and-swaps the per-(work_package_id, type) chain head; second writer rejected with 'head moved' |
| `pull.py` post-append recheck | needed (re-read tip) | redundant — head-move rejection is at write time |
| Affected files | `schema_fragment.json`, `setup_schema.py`, `store.py`, `executing.py`, `pull.py`, plus README + reports |

The formal models in `system-models/` abstract the race resolution as `commitClaim | abortClaim`. The abstraction is mechanism-agnostic, so the assertions still hold without any structural model change.

## Cross-Source Consistency

| Property | Code (post-migration) | Schema | Skill prose | Model | Verdict |
|----------|----------------------|--------|-------------|-------|---------|
| Terminal absorption | `finished.py` refuses if current ∈ {done, rejected}; `cancel.py` refuses cancellation of terminal | n/a | `finished/SKILL.md` documents | `TerminalAbsorbing` ✓ | **Aligned** |
| Proof required for terminal | `finished.py` refuses without report; `cancel_task` synthesizes a TaskReport | n/a | `finished/SKILL.md` documents | `ProofRequiredForTerminal` ✓ | **Aligned** |
| Single owner of latest TaskStatus | `executing.py` exit 8 via `ClaimLost`, raised when `append_status` hits 'head moved' | `prevStatus: chain_predecessor: true` | `executing/SKILL.md` updated this session | `SingleOwner`, `NoDoubleCommit`, `OwnerStableThroughWorkingPhase` ✓ | **Aligned (mechanism shifted to storage)** |
| Dep gate at claim time | `next.py` skips tasks whose `dependsOn` are not all done | n/a | `next/SKILL.md` documents | `DependenciesDoneAtClaim` ✓ | **Aligned** |
| Slug uniqueness | `store.create_task` uses `Task.text = task:<queue>/<slug>`, raises `SlugTaken` on dup `text_sha256` | n/a (pure content-address) | `plan/SKILL.md` documents | `UniqueSlugInQueue` (in `planning_plan_race.als`) ✓ | **Aligned** |
| Verifier gate on `done` | `finished.py` runs verifier, exit 9 on fail | `Task.attributes.verifier` (free string) | `finished/SKILL.md` documents | `VerifierGateOnDone`, `VerifyRequiresWorkingReport` ✓ | **Aligned** |
| Sticky-context coherence | `store.check_sticky_eligibility`; refusal exit 10 across executing/heartbeat/report/finished | n/a | per-skill prose | `StickyChainCoherence`, `StickyBindingOnlyAtClaim` ✓ | **Aligned** |
| Cancellation | `cancel.py` allows non-owner; `store.cancel_task` synthesizes report + rejected status (now via `append_status`, not raw `create_item`) | n/a | `cancel/SKILL.md` documents | `CancelledIsRejectedTerminal`, `CancelOnlyOnNonTerminal`, `CancelledHasProof` ✓ | **Aligned** |
| Reclaim from dead lease | `sweep.py` + `store.reclaim` (now via `append_status`) | n/a | `cancel/SKILL.md`, `sweep` doc | `ReclaimRequiresDeadOwner`, `LiveWorkerActions`, `NoZombieAfterReclaim` ✓ in `planning_lease.als` | **Aligned** |

## Discrepancies

### 1. Mechanism shift: race resolution moved from PM scripts to storage layer
- **Source**: `skills/pm/scripts/store.py:append_claim` (post-migration) — the body is now a thin wrapper around `append_status('working', …)`; the deterministic-text trick (`claim:<task>/<prev>`) is gone. `schema_fragment.json` declares `prevStatus`/`prevReport`/`prevHeartbeat` with `chain_predecessor: true`.
- **Outcome**: **Aligned** — but worth flagging because the *mechanism* changed even though the *property* didn't. The formal model's `commitClaim | abortClaim` abstraction was broad enough to absorb the shift; if it had hard-coded "duplicate text rejected" the migration would have invalidated it.
- **Direction**: No action on the model — its abstraction was correctly chosen. The mechanism is now structurally enforced at a lower layer than before, which is strictly stronger (a `pm`-bypass that calls `create_item` directly is now also bound by the head check, where previously only the deterministic-text gate caught it).
- **Model evidence**:
  - `NoDoubleCommit` in `planning.als:355` ✓ — at most one Attempt per task ever ends up Committed.
  - `SingleOwner` in `planning.als:347` ✓ — `lone t.owner` always.
  - `ClaimRace` run in `planning.als:536` SAT — exhibits the resolution in 8 steps.
- **Source evidence**: `store.py:HeadMoved` exception (new); `_is_head_moved` detector; `append_claim` reduced to `append_status` + `HeadMoved → ClaimLost` translation.
- **Impact**: README and `planning-enforcement.md` updated this session to point at the new gate location. `system-models/reports/cache-staleness-investigation.md` is a historical artifact and intentionally not edited.

### 2. Stale prose in `planning.als` referring to "recheck protocol"
- **Source**: pre-edit `planning.als:18` (header) and `planning.als:213` (`abortClaim` comment) said "recheck protocol catches it". Post-migration the rejection is at write time, not via a separate recheck.
- **Outcome**: **FixSource (model prose)** — the assertions and predicates were correct, but the explanatory comments described an outdated mechanism.
- **Direction**: Already fixed in this session — header now says "chain-head compare-and-swap" and `abortClaim` comment names `chain_predecessor` explicitly.
- **Model evidence**: predicates `commitClaim` and `abortClaim` unchanged structurally — the prose was the only stale element.
- **Source evidence**: `git diff system-models/planning.als` shows two narrative-only edits, no semantic change.
- **Impact**: Reader correctly inferring the current mechanism from the model.

### 3. Stale narrative in `executing/SKILL.md`
- **Source**: pre-edit `skills/pm/executing/SKILL.md:21-22, :38-46` described deterministic-text collision as the race-resolution primitive.
- **Outcome**: **FixSource (skill prose)** — already updated this session to describe the `chain_predecessor` head-move mechanism. Exit codes and worker-loop contract unchanged.
- **Direction**: Done.
- **Impact**: Operators reading the skill file see the current mechanism; exit-8 retry semantics unchanged.

### 4. Slug-race race window — intentional gap, still valid
- **Source**: `planning.als` does not model slug uniqueness; `planning_plan_race.als` is the dedicated companion model.
- **Outcome**: **Exclusion (intentional)** — documented in `planning.als:577-596` (the boundary comment) and reproduced unchanged.
- **Direction**: No action — the migration didn't touch slug semantics. `planning_plan_race.als` still passes 1/1 check, and the witness scenario is correctly UNSAT under the safe `commitPlan` (the mechanism — `text_sha256` dup rejection on `task:<queue>/<slug>`) is unchanged by chain_predecessor.

## Boundary Review

| Element | Decision | Model reference | Source reference | Impact |
|---------|----------|-----------------|-----------------|--------|
| `chain_predecessor` mechanism | Stub (storage-layer concern) | `commitClaim`/`abortClaim` predicates abstract over it | `schema_fragment.json` declares it; `store.HeadMoved` translates it | Model would not benefit from naming the mechanism — abstraction is a feature here |
| Heartbeat chain race | Excluded | n/a — `planning.als` does not model heartbeats | `store.append_heartbeat` now raises `HeadMoved` on stale `prevHeartbeat` | Heartbeat racing is operator-monitoring noise, not a correctness concern; properly out of scope |
| Report chain race | Excluded | n/a — `planning.als` collapses reports to `HasReport` | `store.append_report` now raises `HeadMoved` on stale `prevReport` | If two parallel reports are filed, one loses; current code propagates as runtime error. Acceptable: an owner workflow files reports serially |
| Schema head race (`set_schema`) | Excluded | n/a | `setup_schema.py` now passes `expected_prev` from `get_schema_history` | Single-shot setup operation; concurrent `set_schema` from two operators is a deployment-process question, not a runtime correctness one |
| Cross-skill auto/guided execution | Excluded | n/a | `auto-skill-execution`, `guided-skill-execution` skills | They drive the queue using the verified PM primitives; their decision logic is policy, not protocol |

## Gap Assertions (documented exclusions)

| Gap | Assertion | Why excluded | Risk level |
|-----|-----------|-------------|------------|
| Slug-race in `plan` | None in `planning.als`; covered by `UniqueSlugInQueue` in `planning_plan_race.als` | Two-model split keeps the core protocol model focused on the post-plan lifecycle | Safe — companion model fully covers the property |

## Remaining gaps (not yet modeled)
- **Heartbeat staleness semantics** — `planning_lease.als` models the lease, but heartbeat freshness windows (the sweeper's `idle_seconds` threshold) are policy. Adding them would let us prove "no false reclaim of a heartbeating worker" — currently delegated to operator tuning.
- **Verifier subprocess timeout** — `finished.py` has a 60s default with `--verifier-timeout`; not modeled. A timeout failure is treated as exit 9 (verifier failed); an extended model could distinguish "verifier said no" from "verifier timed out".
- **Sticky-chain descendant walk performance** — `store.collect_required_contexts` walks both ancestors and descendants. The model proves correctness; large-graph performance is a non-functional concern.

## Enforcement Audit (post-migration delta)

| Assertion | Verdict | Decision point | Gate language? | Notes |
|-----------|---------|---------------|----------------|-------|
| `NoDoubleCommit` / `SingleOwner` | **Enforced** (now stronger) | Storage-layer write: `create_item` for type=TaskStatus | "head moved" — structural rejection by `chain_predecessor` rule | Previously enforced by deterministic-text collision; now enforced by the chain-head pointer. Strictly stronger because it covers any direct `create_item` caller, not just `append_claim` writers using the deterministic-text convention. |
| `ProofRequiredForTerminal` | **Enforced** | `finished.py:7` (refuses without report) | "refusing: no TaskReport found" | Unchanged. |
| `TerminalAbsorbing` | **Enforced** | `finished.py:6` (refuses unless current ∈ {working, new}) | "refusing: status is X" | Unchanged. |
| `DependenciesDoneAtClaim` | **Enforced** | `next.py` candidate filter | structural skip | Unchanged. |
| `VerifierGateOnDone` | **Enforced** | `finished.py` exit 9 | "verifier failed (exit N); not finishing" | Unchanged. |
| `StickyChainCoherence` / `StickyBindingOnlyAtClaim` | **Enforced** | `executing.py` exit 10 | "refusing: sticky-context mismatch" | Unchanged. |
| `CancelledIsRejectedTerminal` / `CancelOnlyOnNonTerminal` / `CancelledHasProof` | **Enforced** | `cancel.py` (refuses terminal) + `store.cancel_task` (synthesizes report + status atomically through `append_status`) | structural | Now routed through `append_status` so cancellation also benefits from `chain_predecessor` head-move enforcement on the closing TaskStatus. |
| Reclaim safety | **Enforced** | `sweep.py` + `store.reclaim` (now via `append_status`) | structural | Reclaim-vs-finish race is now also resolved by the chain_predecessor check on `prevStatus`. |

**Summary**: 9/9 properties enforced. The migration tightened the gate location for the claim-race property (storage layer is structurally lower than PM-script-level), and brought cancel + reclaim under the same gate by routing them through `append_status`. No discrepancies require further code changes.

## Verdict

**The models are still actual.** The mechanism-agnostic abstraction in `planning.als` and the dedicated coverage in `planning_plan_race.als` and `planning_lease.als` together still verify the eight protocol properties under the migrated storage primitive. Two prose comments in `planning.als` and one skill file (`executing/SKILL.md`) were stale and have been corrected; no structural model edits were needed.
